desc: Batumi is Georgia's easiest Black Sea city break, mixing a long seafront promenade, subtropical gardens, and a compact old-town core with easy onward access to Adjara's beaches, food scene, and mountain day trips.
hls: Best all-round Georgia beach-city base if you want sea, cafés, nightlife, and easier airport logistics in one place.
Batumi Boulevard gives the city a long, low-effort waterfront that works well for sunset walks and shoulder-season breaks.
The city combines resort energy with a more usable old-town center than many pure beach strips.
Summer is strongest for swimming and nightlife, but spring and early autumn are often better for city-and-coast balance.
Rainfall is real in autumn and winter, so clear-skied beach expectations can mislead outside peak summer.
Works well as a 3-5 night stay, or longer if you want to add botanical garden, Gonio, and Adjara upland day trips.

todo.name: Batumi Boulevard sunset walk
todo.desc: Use the seafront promenade as your first-evening orientation because it ties together the old center, beach mood, and skyline views.

todo.name: Argo cable car for city views
todo.desc: Take the ride late in the day when the city lights, hills, and Black Sea horizon all read best from above.

todo.name: Old Batumi + Piazza Square wander
todo.desc: Keep one unhurried block for the older streets, squares, and café stops rather than treating Batumi only as a beach base.

todo.name: Batumi Botanical Garden half-day
todo.desc: Go when visibility is good because the sea-facing viewpoints matter as much as the plant collections.

todo.name: Gonio Fortress or south-coast stop
todo.desc: Add one short southern outing if you want a change from promenade time without committing to a full mountain day.

todo.name: Adjara food evening
todo.desc: Make room for an Ajarian khachapuri and seafood-heavy meal because Batumi's food identity is one of the destination's real strengths.

wifi.note: Central Batumi apartments, hotels, and cafés are usually strong for remote work, though older stays and stormy weather can still cause dips.

prac.fltNote: Batumi has its own airport, but there is no current nonstop LGW or LCY route. Most UK trips connect via Istanbul or another regional hub, and Batumi International Airport (BUS) is then only a short taxi or bus ride from the center.
prac.currency: Georgian lari (GEL); cards are common in the center, but beach kiosks, market buys, and marshrutka-style transport are easier with some cash.
prac.lang: Georgian; Russian is still common in tourism, and English is increasingly usable in newer hotels, cafés, and activity businesses.
prac.visa: Georgia entry rules vary by nationality, but many visitors get comparatively simple short-stay access; check current passport-specific rules before booking.
alerts: Autumn and winter can be much wetter than casual beach branding suggests.
Peak-summer traffic and nightlife noise are real near the central seafront.
Batumi is easy to arrive into, but mountain or border-side day trips still need weather and route-time slack.

sweet: Late May to early July and September are usually the best balance for Batumi, with warm-enough seafront weather, easier city wandering, and less peak-summer pressure than the height of August.
